source.name: User distilled premium web UI prompt set
source.license_notes: Rules distilled from user-provided prompts for personal knowledge base use. Do not copy external media assets directly.

layout_rules: Use display/body pairings such as Instrument Serif italic + Inter, Instrument Serif italic + Barlow, Readex Pro, Inter, or Geist + Inter.
Hero title line-height should be 0.8 to 0.95 with letter-spacing -0.02em to -0.05em.
Hero font-weight should usually be 400 to 500, not default bold.
Section headings should use text-4xl to text-6xl with tight line-height.
Body copy should stay small but readable, with max-width around 32ch to 64ch.
On desktop, use asymmetry, scale contrast, and whitespace instead of filling every area.
accessibility_notes: Do not make display type so tight that letters collide on mobile.
Avoid low-contrast gray copy on dark or glass backgrounds.
Do not rely only on decorative serif styling for hierarchy.
Check long words and mixed English/Chinese line wrapping.
implementation_hints: Hero heading scale: text-5xl -> md:text-7xl -> lg:text-[5.5rem].
Section heading scale: text-4xl -> md:text-5xl -> lg:text-6xl.
Use clamp() or viewport units for huge type and test mobile overflow.
Use max-width utilities for body copy and avoid full-width paragraphs.
Use Geist + Inter for developer SaaS; use serif italic pairing for luxury/editorial contrast.
avoid_when: same heading size across all sections
desktop type scale forced onto mobile

quality.notes: Distilled from multiple detailed React/Tailwind premium landing page prompts covering glassmorphism, video hero sections, typography, motion, SaaS, portfolio, and editorial layouts.

retrieval.ai_summary: Premium typography and layout rules: use display/body font pairing, very large tight hero type, short memorable lines, body copy max-width 32ch-64ch, editorial whitespace, asymmetry, and responsive clamp/vw sizing. Avoid default bold SaaS headings and wide paragraphs.
